protocol.name: wlr_foreign_toplevel_management_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2018 Ilia Bozhinov
Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee, provided that the above copyright notice appear in all copies and that both that copyright notice and this permission notice appear in supporting documentation, and that the name of the copyright holders not be used in advertising or publicity pertaining to distribution of the software without specific, written prior permission. The copyright holders make no representations about the suitability of this software for any purpose. It is provided "as is" without express or implied warranty.
THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES WITH REGARD TO THIS SOFTWARE, INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS, IN NO EVENT SHALL THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES WHATSOEVER RESULTING FROM LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE OR OTHER TORTIOUS ACTION, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THIS SOFTWARE.

interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1
interface.description:
The purpose of this protocol is to enable some clients such as taskbars or docks to access a list of currently opened toplevels, and request certain actions on them, such as maximizing, etc.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is experimental and backward incompatible changes may be made. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes are done by bumping the version number in the protocol and interface names and resetting the interface version. Once the protocol is to be declared stable, the 'z' prefix and the version number in the protocol and interface names are removed and the interface version number is reset.

event.name: toplevel
event.description:
This event is emitted whenever a new toplevel window is created. It is emitted for all toplevels, regardless of the app that has created them.
All initial details of the toplevel (title, app_id, states, etc.) will be sent immediately after this event via the corresponding events in zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1.

event.name: finished

description.summary: the compositor has finished with the toplevel manager

event.description: This event indicates that the compositor is done sending events to the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1. The server will destroy the object immediately after sending this request, so it will become invalid and the client should free any resources associated with it.

request.name: stop
request.description:
Indicates the client no longer wishes to receive events for new toplevels. However the compositor may emit further toplevel_created events, until the finished event is emitted.
The client must not send any more requests after this one.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroys the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1 object.
This request should be called either when the client does not want to use the manager anymore or after the finished event has been received to allow destruction of the object.

interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1
interface.description:
A zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object represents an opened toplevel window. Each app may have multiple opened toplevels.
Each toplevel has a list of outputs it is visible on, conveyed by the output_enter and output_leave events.

event.name: title
event.description: This event is emitted whenever the title of the toplevel changes.

event.name: app_id
event.description: This event is emitted whenever the app-id of the toplevel changes.

event.name: output_enter
event.description: This event is emitted whenever the toplevel becomes visible on the given output. A toplevel may be visible on multiple outputs.

event.name: output_leave
event.description: This event is emitted whenever the toplevel stops being visible on the given output.

enum.name: state

description.summary: types of states on the toplevel

enum.description: The different states that a toplevel can have. These have the same meaning as the states with the same names defined in xdg-toplevel.

event.name: state
event.description: This event is emitted immediately after the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 is created and whenever the state of the toplevel changes.

event.name: done

description.summary: all information about the toplevel has been sent

event.description:
This event is sent after all changes in the toplevel state have been sent.
This allows changes to the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 properties to be seen as atomic, even if they happen via multiple events.

event.name: closed
event.description:
This event means the toplevel has been destroyed. It is guaranteed that no further events will be emitted for the toplevel.
The compositor must make sure all the existing objects related to the toplevel (e.g. zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1) are destroyed before this event is sent.

event.name: parent
event.description:
This event is emitted whenever the parent of the toplevel changes.
No event is emitted when the toplevel is created with a parent. However, a compositor may send this event with a null parent in order to signify that the toplevel is no longer a child toplevel.

request.name: set_maximized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be maximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be maximized. If the toplevel is already maximized, this request does nothing.

request.name: unset_maximized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be unmaximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unmaximized. If the toplevel is not maximized, this request does nothing.

request.name: set_minimized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be minimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be minimized. If the toplevel is already minimized, this request does nothing.

request.name: unset_minimized

description.summary: requests that the toplevel be unminimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unminimized. If the toplevel is not minimized, this request does nothing.

request.name: activate
request.description: Request that this toplevel be activated on the given seat. There is no guarantee the toplevel will be actually activated.

request.name: close

description.summary: request that the toplevel be closed

request.description: Send a request to the toplevel to close itself. The compositor would typically use a shell-specific method to carry out this request, for example by sending the xdg_toplevel.close event. However, this gives no guarantees the toplevel will actually be destroyed. If and when this happens, the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1.closed event will be emitted.

request.name: set_rectangle

description.summary: the rectangle which represents the toplevel

request.description: The rectangle of the surface specified in this request corresponds to the place where the app using this protocol represents the given toplevel. It can be used by the compositor as a hint for some operations, e.g. minimizing. The client is however not required to set this rectangle.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroys the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object.
This request should be used when the client no longer wants to use the toplevel handle.

request.name: set_fullscreen

description.summary: request that the toplevel be fullscreened

request.description:
Requests that the toplevel be fullscreened on the given output. If the fullscreen state and/or the outputs the client wants are already set, this request has no effect.
The output parameter is only a hint to the compositor. Also, if output is nil, the compositor will choose which output the toplevel will be fullscreened on, if at all.

request.name: unset_fullscreen

description.summary: request that the toplevel be unfullscreened

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unfullscreened. If the toplevel is not fullscreen, this request has no effect.
